data.title: Digital asset infrastructure company Pointsville completes Series A funding round, with Tether participating
data.description: Pointsville, a digital asset infrastructure company, has completed its Series A funding round, led by Valor Capital Group, with participation from Tether, Temasek-backed Superscrypt, SNZ, Credit Saison, K2 Integrity, Citrino, and Dynamo. The specific amount of funding has not yet been disclosed, and the new funds are intended to be used for the company's asset digitization and RWA transformation.

data.title: Yunfeng Financial: In addition to ETH, we also plan to include BTC, SOL, etc. into the company's strategic reserve assets
data.description: Yunfeng Financial previously purchased a total of 10,000 ETH on the open market. Alibaba founder Jack Ma and Yu Feng, chairman of Yunfeng Financial Group, co-founded Yunfeng Fund in 2010. Yunfeng Financial stated that the newly purchased ETH will be listed as an investment asset in its financial statements and that it will continue to increase its investment in digital assets. In addition to Ethereum, it plans to explore the inclusion of diversified mainstream digital assets, including Bitcoin (BTC) and Solana (SOL), in its strategic reserve assets. (Caixin.com)

data.title: Over the past two months, the two addresses have accumulated 22,753 MKR, worth approximately $39.62 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, two addresses received a total of 22,753 MKR from FalconX over the past two months. Address 0xb2c7 received 14,000 MKR worth $24.25 million six hours ago, while address 0xc230 received 8,753 MKR worth $15.37 million over the past two months.

data.title: Thumzup, a company owned by Trump's eldest son, plans to transform into a DOGE mining company and purchase 3,500 DOGE mining machines.
data.description: Thumzup (TZUP), a media company owned by Donald Trump's eldest son, released a shareholder letter announcing its intention to acquire DogeHash Technologies to establish a DOGE mining operation, completing its strategic transformation into a leading cryptocurrency mining company. The company has invested $1 million in Bitcoin, and its board of directors has authorized an increase in holdings of cryptocurrencies including DOGE, LTC, SOL, XRP, ETH, and USDC. It has also authorized the purchase of 2,500 DOGE mining machines, with the potential purchase of an additional 1,000 machines. Thumzup (TZUP) previously announced that it will acquire DogeHash Technologies in an all-stock transaction. The company will be renamed "Dogehash Technologies Holdings, Inc." and trade on the Nasdaq under the new ticker symbol "XDOG." The transaction is expected to close by the end of the year.

data.title: TRON Founder Justin Sun Responds to Token Activity Analysis
data.description: According to Foresight News, TRON founder Justin Sun has addressed a tweet by Nansen CEO Alex Svanevik regarding the activity of WLFI token addresses. Sun responded to the analysis by stating, "I am innocent."

data.title: Anchorage Digital's associated address deposited 5 million ENA to Binance, worth $3.37 million
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the Anchorage Digital address (0x8ab...3780) deposited 5 million ENA (worth $3.37 million) to Binance over the past 17 hours. Currently, the address holds 13.31 million ENA, worth approximately $8.71 million. Anchorage Digital previously partnered with Ethena to introduce USDe to the US market.

data.title: OKX now supports Google Pay
data.description: According to official news, Google Pay has officially launched on the OKX app. Android users in the EU and US can now top up and purchase cryptocurrencies in seconds using this convenient, secure, and reliable payment method. As a long-trusted payment method, Google Pay integration provides greater peace of mind during transactions. In the face of rapidly changing market conditions, users can use Google Pay to more quickly complete their regular investment plans. OKX CEO Star stated that Google Pay integration will provide users with a "simple, secure, and seamless transaction experience."

data.title: A16z-backed Lead Bank hits $1.47 billion valuation with $70 million raise
data.description:
The crypto-friendly lender has completed its $70 million Series B round with participation from a16z.
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/369566/lead-bank-70-million-raise?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: Hong Kong-listed company Ying Yuzhou invested HK$300 million to subscribe to Guofu Quantum shares to promote the development of the Web3 ecosystem.
data.description: According to an announcement from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, Hong Kong-listed company Ying Yuzhou announced that it would spend HK$300 million to subscribe to Guofu Quantum shares at a discount to promote the construction of the Web 3 ecosystem. It is reported that the two parties have entered into a subscription agreement. Guofu Quantum has conditionally agreed to issue a total of approximately 169 million subscription shares to the company at a subscription price of HK$1.78 per share. It is reported that after the completion of the transaction, both parties will cooperate with the Hong Kong government's policy to promote the development of the digital economy and make strategic investments in the field of financial technology.

data.title: Economist Predicts Potential Revisions in August Employment Data
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Bloomberg economist Anna Wong has forecasted potential adjustments to the upcoming non-farm payroll report. Wong suggests that regardless of the initial employment figures, they are likely to be revised upwards. She attributes this to typically lower initial response rates in August, often due to factors such as vacations. Historically, subsequent revisions in the following months tend to show an increase.

data.title: Major WLFI Holder Distributes Tokens Across Multiple Addresses
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on-chain analyst Ai Yi (@ai_9684xtpa) has observed significant activity from the largest individual holder of WLFI tokens. Over the past 18 hours, this holder has distributed 79.54 million WLFI tokens, valued at approximately $14.69 million, across nine different addresses. One of these addresses, moonmanifestphilanthropy.eth, had a minor sell-off three days ago, but currently, none of the receiving addresses have engaged in selling activities.

The top holder, associated with the address moonmanifest.eth, possesses 1% of the WLFI tokens. During the Token Generation Event (TGE), 200 million tokens were unlocked. This address is suspected to belong to TechnoRevenant, a key participant in the XPL hedging event.

data.title: Dogecoin may see first-ever ETF launch next week: Analyst
data.description:
REX Shares is taking the same regulatory route for its Dogecoin ETF as it did to get its Solana staking ETF over the line.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/dogecoin-etf-united-states-launch-next-week-analyst?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: After PTB was listed on MGBX, it rose above 0.046901 USDT, with a 24-hour increase of over 67%.
data.description: According to Odaily's Planet Daily, MGBX spot market data shows that PTB has risen above 0.046901 USDT, currently trading at 0.04431 USDT. This represents a 24-hour increase of over 67%, a cumulative increase of over 113%, and a circulating market capitalization exceeding $100 million. Portal To Bitcoin (PTB) is dedicated to building Bitcoin as a settlement layer for both on-chain and real-world markets. Its core BitScaler technology supports trust minimization, accelerating transactions and reducing fees. PTB is a Web3 cross-chain solution with blockchain settlement capabilities.

data.title: iSpecimen, a biospecimen procurement company, plans to acquire SOL for $200 million and establish a treasury.
data.description: iSpecimen Inc. (ISPC), a biospecimen procurement company, announced plans to build a $200 million digital asset trove, aiming to diversify its balance sheet and support growth initiatives through tokenized real-world assets and leading cryptocurrencies. iSpecimen is continuing parallel discussions to develop a financial plan based on the Solana blockchain. The company plans to purchase Solana tokens (SOL) through over-the-counter transactions with institutional cryptocurrency institutions, including locked Solana tokens with contractual transfer or vesting restrictions. (Investing)

data.title: Institutional Solana holdings rose to 8.887 million, accounting for 1.55% of the current total supply of SOL.
data.description: According to Strategic SOL Reserve data, 13 entities have established SOL treasury reserves, collectively holding 8.887 million SOL, representing 1.55% of the current total SOL supply. Of this, approximately 585,000 SOL are staked, valued at $104.1 million, with an average staking yield of 6.86%, representing 0.102% of the total supply. The entities with the largest holdings are as follows: Sharps Technology (STSS) ranks first, currently holding 2.14 million SOL, worth approximately US$435 million, and its holdings have not changed in the past 30 days; DeFi Development Corp (DFDV) ranks second, currently holding 2.028 million SOL, worth approximately US$412 million, and its holdings have increased by 5.2% in the past 30 days; Upexi (UPXI) ranks third, currently holding 2 million SOL, worth approximately US$406 million, and its holdings have increased by 15.3% in the past 30 days; Mercurity Fintech (MFH) ranks fourth, currently holding 1.083 million SOL, worth approximately US$220.4 million, and its holdings have not changed in the past 30 days; iSpecimen Inc (ISPC) ranks fifth, currently holding 1 million SOL, worth approximately US$215.1 million, and its holdings have increased by 8.7% in the past 30 days; SOL Strategies Inc (CYFRF) ranks sixth, currently holding 403,000 SOL, worth approximately US$82 million, with a 12.4% increase in holdings in the past 30 days.

data.title: South Korea Introduces Guidelines for Virtual Asset Lending Services
data.description:
According to PANews, South Korea's financial regulatory authorities have introduced the first set of guidelines for virtual asset lending services. In response to increased investor risks due to intensified competition among exchanges, the regulations prohibit leverage and cash lending, set personal limits, and cap fees to prevent short-selling activities. On September 5, the Financial Services Commission announced the implementation of the self-regulatory 'Virtual Asset Lending Business Guidelines,' developed by the Financial Supervisory Service and DAXA.

The new guidelines focus on three core areas: service scope limitations, user protection, and market stability. They explicitly ban excessive leverage lending and cash lending in Korean won, requiring exchanges to use their own assets for services and prohibiting third-party delegation or indirect lending models.

In terms of enhanced user protection measures, first-time users must complete DAXA's online education and adaptability test. Loan limits are set between 30 million to 70 million Korean won, depending on trading experience. Users must be notified in advance of any forced liquidation risks and allowed to add collateral. The annual fee rate is capped at 20%, and exchanges must publicly disclose the lending status and liquidation cases for each cryptocurrency.

For market stability, the guidelines restrict lending targets to the top 20 market cap assets or those listed on at least three Korean won exchanges, excluding assets under trading warnings or suspected of abnormal trading. An internal control mechanism is required to prevent excessive concentration in specific cryptocurrencies, which could lead to market volatility.

data.title: 30 minutes ago, a whale donated 10 BTC to a Ukrainian address.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, 30 minutes ago, a whale donated 10 BTC, equivalent to approximately US$1.108 million, to a Ukrainian-related address.

data.title: SEC Announces Shift in Crypto Regulatory Agenda
data.description:
SEC reveals new crypto regulatory agenda, proposing trading on national securities exchanges, safe harbors, reducing compliance burden.
source: https://coincu.com/360088-sec-crypto-regulatory-agenda

data.title: Pointsville Secures Series A Funding for Digital Asset Expansion
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, digital asset infrastructure company Pointsville has announced the completion of its Series A funding round. The round was led by Valor Capital Group, with participation from Tether, Superscrypt backed by Temasek, SNZ, Credit Saison, K2 Integrity, Citrino, and Dynamo. The specific amount raised has not been disclosed. The newly acquired funds are intended to support the company's efforts in asset digitization and transitioning to real-world asset (RWA) integration.

Tether is set to collaborate with Pointsville to provide the necessary support for integrating real-world assets and loyalty programs into the digital economy.
